# SRC005 - Advertising Creativity Meta-Analysis

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Advertising Effectiveness |
| Authors / source | Rosengren, Eisend, Koslow & Dahlen |
| Year | 2020 |
| Source type | Meta-analysis |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/0022242920929288](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/0022242920929288) |

## Bias / limitation

Academic; abstract/paywall limits access.

## Core takeaway

Creativity can improve responses, but the effect depends on dimensions and context.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to separate “creative noise” from strategically useful distinctiveness.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC005
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
